criteria.name: habit_controls_keyboard_reachable
criteria.description: Every interactive control — New Habit fields, category chips, steppers, one-tap complete, drag handle, Pause/Resume, Export as JSON, Import, and Load Malformed Sample — is reachable with keyboard Tab and activatable with Enter or Space, and shows a clearly visible focus indicator
criteria.reasoning: yes — Native fields and buttons are tabbable, and keyboard focus shows a visible 3px outline. Evidence: habit_controls_keyboard_reachable-shared.png

criteria.name: import_dialog_traps_and_restores_focus
criteria.description: The Import confirmation dialog traps focus while open and returns focus to the invoking Import control on Cancel or Confirm close
criteria.reasoning: yes — Tab cycled between dialog controls, and Cancel closed the dialog and restored focus to Import from JSON. Evidence: import_dialog_traps_and_restores_focus-shared.png

criteria.name: chrome_icons_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: App chrome icons (Phosphor set) used as controls expose accessible names via aria-label or equivalent text, and decorative icons are hidden from assistive tech
criteria.reasoning: yes — Control icons expose aria-labels, while decorative SVG icons use aria-hidden="true". Evidence: semantic_buttons_for_checkin_and_filters-shared.png

criteria.name: recovery_and_malformed_use_alert_live_region
criteria.description: The recovery notice and Load Malformed Sample outcome are announced through a role=alert live region without requiring focus to move to them
criteria.reasoning: yes — Loading the malformed sample produced visible assertive role=alert live regions without moving focus. Evidence: recovery_and_malformed_use_alert_live_region-shared.png

criteria.name: new_habit_fields_explicitly_labeled
criteria.description: New Habit form fields (name, icon, targetType, targetCount, category, reminder) and category-create name use explicit labels programmatically associated with their inputs; inline field-contract errors are adjacent to and associated with the named field
criteria.reasoning: yes — Habit name, target count, category, reminder, target groups, icon group, and category-create name are explicitly labeled; validation errors are adjacent and associated via aria-describedby. Evidence: new_habit_fields_explicitly_labeled-shared.png

criteria.name: view_headings_logical_order
criteria.description: View titles for habits, Stats, Heatmap, and Import/Data use a logical heading order without skipping levels (for example H1 then H2)
criteria.reasoning: yes — Habits, Stats, Heatmap, and Data views use H1 followed by H2, with nested H3 sections where present. Evidence: view_headings_logical_order-shared.png

criteria.name: main_landmark_for_habit_shell
criteria.description: The habit list / Stats / Heatmap shell exposes a main landmark (or equivalent) so keyboard and assistive-tech users can jump past chrome
criteria.reasoning: yes — The shell exposes a native main landmark containing the primary content. Evidence: habit_controls_keyboard_reachable-shared.png

criteria.name: habit_text_and_controls_contrast
criteria.description: Primary text (#1B2430 on #F4F7F6/#FFFFFF), muted secondary text, primary buttons (#0F9D74 with white text), and category chips maintain sufficient contrast for readable labels and active states
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Primary green button text measured about 3.45:1 against #0F9D74, below the usual 4.5:1 threshold for normal-sized labels. Evidence: habit_text_and_controls_contrast-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_buttons_for_checkin_and_filters
criteria.description: Check-in, stepper, category filter chips, Pause/Resume, Export as JSON, and Import use semantic button (or equivalent role=button) controls rather than bare non-interactive divs
criteria.reasoning: yes — Check-in, filters, pause/resume, drag handle, export, import, and related controls are implemented as native buttons; the target count uses a native number input. Evidence: semantic_buttons_for_checkin_and_filters-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_drops_celebration
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, decorative animations and the streak-milestone confetti celebration are removed or reduced while check-in state, flame treatment, and list membership still update instantly and correctly
criteria.reasoning: yes — With prefers-reduced-motion enabled, check-in updated immediately, no confetti appeared, and the card animation duration was reduced to 1e-06s. Evidence: reduced_motion_drops_celebration-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).
criteria.reasoning: yes — Headings consistently use sentence case across the visible views. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs such as Add habit, Export as JSON, and Load Malformed Sample, not generic Submit or OK when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The quick-tour dismissal uses the generic label “Got it” where a more specific action label could be used. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders field-contract validation errors for New Habit, category create, or Import, they name the offending field and the rule (for example name must be non-empty, targetCount must be 1–100, schemaVersion must be loopdaily.workspace.v1), not just Invalid.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Validation messages identify the field and rule, including “name must be non-empty.” Evidence: errors_name_fix-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add it.
criteria.reasoning: yes — The empty state explains that habits belong there and directs users to create their first habit. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Body copy is generally clear and grammatical, with a visible agreement error: “1 valid categories.” Evidence: import-error-shared.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent (not 'task' here and 'todo' there).
criteria.reasoning: yes — The app consistently uses “habit,” “category,” “completion,” and “workspace” for the same concepts. Evidence: habits-data-shared.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, dates, and units, formatting is consistent (same date format, same decimal precision, units labeled).
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The app mixes formats such as “July completions,” “Jul 10,” “0d streak,” and “0 day streak.” Evidence: stats-shared.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened ('Entry saved'), not vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: yes — Success feedback explicitly states what happened, including “Malformed sample loaded” and the number of habits recovered. Evidence: import-error-shared.png
